raw_consumer_statement:
My Citi credit card was stolen and used fraudulently in XXXX last year XXXX. I disputed these charges with Citi and they refused to acknowledge it and still chases me for the bill. 

Citi has refused to deal with this after emailing and calling them hundreds of times over the past year and I am considering legal action as they pursue this bill. Citi is in violation of 5 laws : Truth in Lending Act / Fair Credit Billing Act ( Reg Z ). I get billing error rights : Citi must acknowledge my dispute within 30 days and resolve within two billing cycles ( 90 days ) ; while its pending they cant try to collect the disputed amount, add related finance/late charges to it, or report it as delinquentand they cant demand a police report or sworn affidavit just to consider it. 

Unauthorized-use cap. By law my liability for an unauthorized credit-card charge is at most {$50.00} ( many issuers make it {$0.00} ). Theyre trying to stick me with a fraud charge, TILA 1643/ Reg Z 1026.12 ( b ).

FCRA ( credit reporting ). Once I disputed through a credit bureau, Citi ( as a furnisher
) must conduct a reasonable investigation, correct/update what they report, and note the account as disputed. Refusing to investigate after a bureau notifies them violates FCRA 1681s-2 ; CFPB has warned furnishers on this.

FDCPA / Regulation F ( if a third-party collector is chasing it ). The collector has to send a validation notice, and if I dispute in writing within 30 days they must cease collection until they mail verification.

UDAAP ( Dodd-Frank ). Misrepresenting my rights or unfairly continuing fees/collections despite a proper dispute can be an unfair or deceptive act or practice.
